senators.name: Lindsey Graham
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of South Carolina and J.D. from University of South Carolina School of Law. Air Force JAG officer. Career as attorney, state legislator, and U.S. Representative before Senate. Deep expertise in judiciary and foreign affairs. Skilled legislative dealmaker but approach is more instinct-driven than evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of South Carolina School of Law

senators.name: Tim Scott
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. from Charleston Southern University. Career in insurance and financial services. First Black U.S. Senator from South Carolina since Reconstruction. Business background but policy approach is more values-driven than analytically rigorous.

SC-01.name: Nancy Mace
SC-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from The Citadel (first woman to graduate) and M.A. from University of Georgia. Career as business consultant and state legislator. The Citadel provides military-style analytical discipline. However, governance approach is more media-focused than evidence-based.
education: B.A., The Citadel (first woman to graduate)

SC-02.name: Joe Wilson
SC-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Washington and Lee University and J.D. from University of South Carolina School of Law. Career as attorney and state senator. Long-tenured in Congress. Legal background but approach is not distinctly analytical.
education: J.D., University of South Carolina School of Law

SC-03.name: Sheri Biggs
SC-03.notNerdReason: Nurse practitioner by career. Healthcare background provides clinical analytical skills. New member of Congress (2025). Healthcare credentials are STEM-adjacent but legislative record too new to assess evidence-based governance approach.

SC-04.name: William Timmons
SC-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from George Washington University and J.D. from University of South Carolina School of Law. Career as attorney and state senator. Legal training provides analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of South Carolina School of Law

SC-05.name: Ralph Norman
SC-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Presbyterian College. Career in real estate development and state legislature. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

SC-06.name: Jim Clyburn
SC-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from South Carolina State University. Career as civil rights leader, teacher, and state government official. Former House Majority Whip (highest-ranking Black member of Congress in history). Decades of deep legislative expertise. Institutional knowledge is exceptional but approach is more relationship-driven than analytically rigorous.

SC-07.name: Russell Fry
SC-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from Winthrop University and J.D. from Charleston School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Legal training provides basic analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
